name: Scandinavian Pot Roast
recipeIngredient: 12 oz. beer or 1 1/2 cups beef broth
1/2 cup sour cream or plain low-fat yogurt (optional)
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat 2 tablespoons oil in a 4 to 5 quart ovenproof pot over medium heat.
2. Pat the meat dry with paper towels. Add it to the hot oil along with the onion.
3. Brown the meat on all sides, stirring occasionally.
4. Add beer and cider vinegar.
5. Add brown sugar and seasonings (allspice, pepper, dillweed).
6. Cover tightly and bake for 2 1/2 to 3 hours in a preheated oven at 325°F until the meat is fork tender.
7. Lift the meat to a cutting board. Cover loosely with foil and let stand 10 to 15 minutes.
8. Remove bay leaves and skim fat from the sauce.
9. If desired, thicken the sauce by stirring in half a cup of sour cream or plain low-fat yogurt mixed with 3 tablespoons of flour.
10. Slice the meat across the grain and serve with the sauce.
